framework.name: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
framework.issuing_body: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (Government of India); enforced by the Data Protection Board of India
framework.effective_date: 2023-08-11 (enactment); operative provisions implemented via notified rules / notifications
framework.scope: India's national personal data protection law for digital personal data. It applies to processing of digital personal data in India and to certain processing outside India when connected to offering goods or services to individuals in India. For AI systems, it functions as a governance baseline wherever an AI system processes personal data, requiring lawful basis, notice, consent management, security safeguards, breach reporting, and stronger controls for children and significant data fiduciaries.
framework.applies_to: Any organization processing digital personal data in India; also extraterritorial processing tied to offering goods or services to individuals in India. In practice this covers most consumer, employee, platform, SaaS, fintech, health, telecom, and AI/ML systems that ingest or infer from personal data, regardless of company size; enhanced duties can apply to entities notified as Significant Data Fiduciaries.
framework.penalties: Enforced by the Data Protection Board of India. The Act's Schedule sets monetary penalties up to INR 250 crore for failure to maintain reasonable security safeguards, INR 200 crore for breach notification failures, INR 200 crore for children's-data obligations, INR 150 crore for Significant Data Fiduciary obligations, INR 50 crore for any other provision, and INR 10,000 for breach of Data Principal duties.

control.statement: Determine and document whether the AI system's processing of digital personal data falls within the Act's territorial and subject-matter scope.
Process personal data only for a lawful purpose under the Act, using consent or a permitted legitimate use.
Provide clear notice before or at consent collection, including data categories, processing purpose, rights, and complaint mechanism.
Obtain valid, specific, informed, unambiguous, affirmative consent and support easy withdrawal and consent-manager handling where used.
Maintain organizational accountability for processor use, data quality for consequential decisions, security safeguards, breach notification, retention/erasure, DPO contact, and grievance redressal.
If the AI system processes children's data, obtain verifiable parental or guardian consent and avoid tracking, behavioural monitoring, and targeted advertising to children.
If designated a Significant Data Fiduciary, appoint an India-based DPO, engage an independent auditor, and perform periodic DPIAs and audits.
Support data principal rights to access, correction, erasure, grievance redressal, and nomination through operational procedures and system capabilities.
Ensure users exercising rights or providing personal data do not impersonate others, submit false complaints, or provide non-authentic information.
Be prepared for Data Protection Board inquiries, directions, and monetary penalties, including preservation of evidence and remediation tracking.
